sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER, timestamp_col TIME, float_col FLOAT);
SELECT id, skewness(float_col + 1.0) AS skewed_addition FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING skewness(float_col + 1.0) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, skewness(float_col + (timestamp_col + INTERVAL 2 HOUR)) AS complex_skew FROM test_data WHERE skewness(float_col + (timestamp_col + INTERVAL 2 HOUR)) > 0;
WITH ranked_data AS (SELECT id, skewness(float_col) OVER (PARTITION BY id) AS skew_value FROM test_data) SELECT id, skew_value FROM ranked_data WHERE skew_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t1.id, skewness(float_col + 1.0) AS skewed, t2.timestamp_col FROM test_data t1 JOIN test_data t2 ON t1.id = t2.id + 1 WHERE t1.float_col IS NOT NULL;
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE float_col + 2.0 > 0) AS positive_count, skewness(float_col + 2.0) AS skew_value FROM test_data GROUP BY float_col HAVING skew_value IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE temp_table AS SELECT id, float_col FROM test_data WHERE timestamp_col IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, skewness(float_col + CASE WHEN timestamp_col IS NOT NULL THEN INTERVAL 1 HOUR ELSE INTERVAL 0 HOUR END) AS skew_case FROM temp_table GROUP BY id;
SELECT id, skewness(NULLIF(float_col, 0) + 10.0) AS skew_with_null FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING skew_with_null < 1.5;
